Chip Industry Technical Paper Roundup: July 21

3

Shares

3

Industry Research

Chip Industry Technical Paper Roundup: July 21

3

Shares

3

Near-memory HBM for LLM inference; processing-in-memory for 3D DRAM; SDV hardware abstraction; 3nm GAAFET SRAM self-heating and rad-hard; monolithic CMOS-photonic integration; quantum photonic chips; AI-driven thermal modeling for 3D photonic circuits.

July 21st, 2026 -

By: Linda Christensen

New technical papers recently added to Semiconductor Engineering’s library:

Technical Paper
Research Organizations

StreamDQ: Near-Memory Weight DeQuantization in Custom HBM for Scalable AI Inference Acceleration
SK hynix

Open DRAM Model—Part II: Enabling Processing-in-Memory in 3-D DRAM
Georgia Institute of Technology

Evaluating Hardware Abstraction Layer Concepts for Software Defined Vehicles: Insights into Applicability and Effectiveness
University of Stuttgart

Self-Heating and Radiation Hardness Studies of 3nm GAA-FET-Based SRAM with Different Substrate Isolation Techniques
San Jose State University, Sandia National Laboratories

Monolithic Integration of Piezo-Optomechanical Photonics and CMOS Electronics
MITRE, University of Colorado Boulder, Sandia National Laboratories, University of Arizona, MIT

Design and Benchmarking of a Quantum Photonic Chip
Rotonium, National University of Singapore, Inveriant, Politecnico di Milano, CNIT

AI-Driven Thermal Mapping and Management in 3D Integrated Photonic Circuits
University of Florida, ficonTEC Service, Colorado State University

 
Find more semiconductor research papers here.
 

3

Shares

3

Tags: Centre for Quantum Technologies CNIT Colorado State University ficonTEC Service Georgia Institute of Technology Inveriant Massachusetts Institute of Technology MITRE National University of Singapore Politecnico di Milano Rotonium San Jose State University Sandia National Laboratories semiconductor research SK Hynix University of Arizona University of Colorado Boulder University of Florida University of Stuttgart

Linda Christensen   (all posts)

Linda Christensen is vice president of operations and a contributing writer at Semiconductor Engineering.
